text:
Once upon a time, there was a curious little squirrel named Sparkle. She lived near a big oak tree that was famous for its golden apples. Every day, Sparkle would scamper up that tree and collect tiny morsels of magic gold dust. Every time she did, she whispered a silly song about a squirrel who loves apples.

One day, while chasing a mischievous moth, Sparkle forgot she had lots of gold dust in her pouch. Down she went, tumbling onto a road.

"Oh no!" she thought. "I've fallen! But how did I ever get here?" Then she noticed her reflection – not a golden shimmer this way, but a silly streak of rainbow dust.

She was so disappointed. How could she have forgotten something so important?

Then, passing an old woman selling feather dusters, Sparkle had an idea. Could she use this duster to collect dust?

She worked quickly. Dust billowed from the duster, sparkling through the trees
